name: Benjamin Harrison
portraitCredit: Portrait via Wikimedia Commons. Public domain.

bio.scholar: Benjamin Harrison, the twenty-third President, was the grandson of President William Henry Harrison, making them the only grandfather-grandson presidential pair in American history. Nicknamed 'Little Ben' and 'Kid Gloves Harrison,' he presided over a period of industrial expansion and advanced protective tariff policies supporting American manufacturing. His administration also pursued Pacific expansion efforts, including discussions about Hawaiian annexation.
bio.explorer: Benjamin Harrison was the twenty-third president, and his grandfather William Henry Harrison had also been president! They're the only grandfather-grandson pair to both become president. During his time, America's factories and businesses were growing fast. He worked to protect American businesses by putting taxes on goods from other countries.

text.scholar: Led during a period of significant industrial expansion and economic growth in the United States.
text.explorer: He was president during a time when American factories and businesses were booming.

sources.citation: Miller Center: benjamin harrison - Key Events

text.scholar: Advanced protective tariff policies supporting American manufacturing, signing the McKinley Tariff of 1890.
text.explorer: He signed laws that put taxes on foreign goods to help American factories compete.

sources.citation: Miller Center: benjamin harrison - Key Events

text.scholar: Pursued Pacific expansion and oversaw early Hawaiian annexation discussions, expanding American influence in the Pacific.
text.explorer: He started talks about making Hawaii part of the United States and grew America's influence in the Pacific Ocean.

sources.citation: Miller Center: benjamin harrison - Key Events

text.scholar: Harrison was the grandson of President William Henry Harrison (9th president), making them the only grandfather-grandson pair to both serve as president.
text.explorer: His grandpa was also a president! They're the only grandfather-grandson duo to both become president.

sources.citation: Miller Center: benjamin harrison - Key Events

text.scholar: He was raised on a farm next to his grandfather's vast estate in North Bend, Ohio.
text.explorer: He grew up on a farm right next to his grandfather's big property in Ohio.

sources.citation: Miller Center: benjamin harrison - Key Events

text.scholar: Nicknamed 'Little Ben' and 'Kid Gloves Harrison,' he was known for his formal, reserved manner.
text.explorer: People called him 'Little Ben' and 'Kid Gloves Harrison' because he was very formal and proper.
